name: Ratatouille Pie (Litton)
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons seasoned bread crumbs, dry and finely ground
1/2 pound (2 small) zucchini, cut into 1/4-inch slices diagonally
1 small onion, cut in half then cut into 1/4-inch strips
1 container frozen Breaded Eggplant Slices (see recipe)
2 ounces pitted black olives, sliced, optional
recipeInstructions:
1. Generously spray or "butter" a 9-inch pie plate with vegetable oil spray or margarine. Coat the sides and bottom with bread crumbs, leaving any extra crumbs on the bottom of the plate.
2. Place zucchini in a 1-quart casserole dish; cover. Microwave at High for 2 to 4 minutes, or until tender-crisp, stirring once or twice. Drain.
3. Layer zucchini in pie plate along the sides and bottom (layers will overlap). Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese. Set aside.
4. In a medium bowl, combine onion, green pepper, garlic, and olive oil; cover. Microwave at High for 3 to 5 minutes, or until vegetables are tender. Drain.
5. Stir in tomato sauce, oregano, and pepper. Spoon over zucchini.
6. Top with frozen eggplant slices. Microwave at High for 7 to 12 minutes, or until hot, rotating the plate once or twice. Let stand 2 minutes.
7. Garnish with sliced black olives if desired.
Tomato Sauce:
- Use 1 can (8 oz.) tomato sauce plus 1/4 teaspoon sugar, 1/8 teaspoon dried basil leaves, and 1/8 teaspoon onion powder.
